name: InvestEdge
oneLiner: Microservices-based stock broker simulation with real-time market data, order execution, portfolio tracking, and notifications.
description: Built a stock broker platform inspired by Zerodha and Upstox, focusing on backend service ownership, real-time communication, broker API integration, risk validation, and trading workflow safety.
highlights: Built real-time market data streaming with live price updates, watchlists, charts, portfolio valuation, and trading dashboard integration
Designed clear service boundaries across auth, market data, watchlist, order manager, risk management, portfolio, reports, user manager, and notifications
Implemented broker-style order flow with gRPC-based RMS validation before execution, sandbox/live execution separation, and local order ledger persistence
Built backend-driven portfolio, holdings, trade reports, realized P&L, unrealized P&L, and execution-mode filtering
Integrated Firebase Cloud Messaging for browser notifications on order placement, cancellation, RMS rejection, and test events

name: HireSight
oneLiner: AI-powered SaaS platform for resume scoring, job matching, and interview simulation.
description: Developed a full-stack career platform for students and freshers with resume analysis, job compatibility scoring, interview simulation, and AI-generated feedback.
highlights: Built resume analysis, job matching, interview simulation, resume history, compatibility scoring, and AI-generated feedback workflows
Implemented JWT cookie authentication, Google OAuth, PDF upload handling, and protected dashboard routes
Designed session-safe interview submission to prevent partial database writes when users abandon interviews
Separated structured auth data from flexible resume, job, and interview records using PostgreSQL and MongoDB
Tested complete user flows across auth, resume upload, job matching, interview start, submission, feedback, and review

name: MultiSource RAG Assistant
oneLiner: Production-style GenAI application for grounded question answering over multiple source types.
description: Built a Retrieval-Augmented Generation assistant that ingests user-provided knowledge sources, stores embeddings in a vector database, retrieves relevant chunks, and generates grounded answers with visible source evidence.
highlights: Supported ingestion from raw text, PDF, TXT, Markdown, DOCX, PPTX, website URLs, and YouTube transcripts
Implemented the complete RAG pipeline with text extraction, cleaning, chunking, embedding generation, pgvector storage, semantic retrieval, and grounded LLM answering
Returned visible source evidence with source type, source name, chunk index, similarity score, retrieved text, and collection statistics
Built collection-based workspaces for separating knowledge bases such as DSA notes, system design notes, and GenAI interview material
Added dashboard features including collection selector, ingestion panel, evidence panel, local memory, activity log, backend health status, and duplicate source feedback

name: Terminal Resume
oneLiner: Browser-based terminal emulator showcasing an interactive CLI-driven portfolio.
description: Built a fully interactive terminal interface replicating real shell behavior with structured command parsing, execution context, async output streaming, and session persistence.
highlights: Registry-based command architecture with strict separation of data and logic
Unified async output engine with streaming text and theme-aware rendering
Autocomplete, history navigation, typo suggestions, limited flags, and man pages
Session-aware progressive boot sequence
Grounded AI-powered ask command using constrained prompting

name: ClerkMate
oneLiner: CLI-first productivity tool for structured standup logging with AI summaries.
description: Built a terminal-first productivity system with offline-first local storage, backend sync, and centralized dashboard.
highlights: Modular CLI architecture with command registry
Offline-first design with backend synchronization
AI-powered weekly summaries with fingerprint-based deduplication
Secure authentication with well-defined API contracts
